title: World News Today Live Updates on July 15, 2026 : Did Iran track US troops? Tehran allegedly exploited mobile networks during war: Report
content:
• The news update on July 15, 2026, highlighted a report alleging that Iran tracked US troops by exploiting mobile networks during war.
• Livemint's 'Personal finance' coverage includes insights into mutual funds, small savings schemes (like PPF, SSY, post office savings scheme), top bank FDs, personal loans, credit cards, real estate, and income tax.
• Livemint's 'Economy' coverage provides in-depth analysis of India's macro and micro-economic indicators such as GDP, inflation, forex, fiscal deficit, current account deficit, interest rate cycle, RBI circulars, indirect taxes, GST, and Insolvency and Bankruptcy.

title: Did Iran track US troops? Tehran allegedly exploited mobile networks during war: Report
content:
• Iran-linked actors allegedly exploited vulnerabilities in the decades-old Signalling System No. 7 (SS7) telecommunications protocol and commercial smartphone advertising databases to track US troops.
• The alleged surveillance campaign, detailed in a Financial Times report, began after an Israeli military operation against Iran in late February.
• The US Central Command (CENTCOM) warned Congress in April about adversary exploitation of commercial location data to target US personnel and stated it had implemented 'unprecedented force-protection measures'.

title: ‘France played poorly because…’: Social media tears apart Kylian Mbappe’s team after defeat to Spain | World Cup 2026
content:
• France was eliminated from the FIFA World Cup 2026 after losing to Spain.
• Kylian Mbappe, captain of the French team, admitted his team underperformed during their defeat to Spain.
• French President Emmanuel Macron congratulated Spain on their qualification and acknowledged France's defeat in the FIFA World Cup 2026 match.

title: Elon Musk may have broken Wisconsin’s bribery law by promising voters $1 million payouts during Supreme Court election
content:
• Tesla CEO Elon Musk faces potential criminal charges in Wisconsin for allegedly violating the state's election bribery law by offering $1 million checks to voters during the 2025 state Supreme Court election.
• The 2025 Wisconsin Supreme Court election, where Elon Musk backed Brad Schimel, was the most expensive judicial race in US history with spending estimated well over $100 million.
• The Wisconsin Elections Commission voted 5-1 to refer complaints against Elon Musk to the Brown County district attorney's office for probable cause of breaking the state's election bribery law.

title: ICE suspends most vehicle stops after fatal shootings in Maine and Texas: Report
content:
• The United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has suspended most traffic stops nationwide, a directive issued after fatal shootings in Maine and Texas.
• On Monday (13 July), an ICE agent fatally shot 26-year-old Joan Sebastian Guerrero, a Colombian national, during a traffic stop in Biddeford, Maine.
• The shooting in Maine marks at least the eleventh fatal shooting involving an ICE or Border Patrol officer since President Trump returned to office last year.

title: Chasing AI jobs for 30% salary hike? Report says don't ignore this sector offering 50% increment
content:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has tightened its BRSR Core mandates in 2026, requiring top-listed companies to conduct rigorous ESG audits.
• According to the Michael Page India Salary Guide 2026, Property & Construction, specifically ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) focused roles, are commanding the highest salary increments of 35% to 50%.
• In 2026, professionals specializing in AI, Machine Learning, semiconductors, and battery technologies can command salary increases of up to 30% when changing jobs.

title: Wholesale inflation hits record 9.9% in June on fuel, food prices
content:
• Wholesale inflation hit a record 9.9% in June.
• The revised Wholesale Price Index (WPI) series now uses 2022-23 as its base year.
• Food inflation, which accounts for around a quarter of the basket, rose to an 18-month high of 6.1% in June, up from 4.5% in May.

title: Govt issues norms to implement global outreach initiative for branding, labelling, export packaging
content:
• The government's global outreach initiative for branding, labelling, and export packaging was announced under the Rs 25,060-crore export promotion mission.
• The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) issued guidelines for this initiative, aiming to develop a unified "Brand India" framework to expand global markets for Indian goods and services.
• The DGFT has sought stakeholder views on the new guidelines, with comments to be submitted within 30 days from the date of issuance of the notice.

title: India-UK FTA explained: How the deal could affect your job, take-home pay and shopping
content:
• The India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) is expected to boost Indian sectors such as textiles, leather, and engineering through lower tariffs.
• Eligible Indian professionals working in the UK could retain more of their take-home pay under the provisions of the India-UK FTA.
• The India-UK FTA may lead to British imports like cars and whisky gradually becoming cheaper for consumers in India.

title: India-UK trade pact comes into force on Wednesday; bilateral trade to hit USD 100 bn by 2030
content:
• The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) comes into force from July 15, aiming to boost bilateral trade to USD 100 billion by 2030.
• The UK Double Contribution Convention (DCC), effective from July 15 as part of CETA, will save over USD 600 million annually by exempting more than 75,000 Indian workers from UK social security contributions for up to 5 years.
• Under CETA from July 15, India's export sectors such as textiles, leather, and gems will enter the British market at zero duty; additionally, India imported nearly USD 5.2 billion worth of silver from the UK in FY26, and its current 10.75% import duty will be eliminated over 10 years.
